$comment: This schema is normatively defined in docs/aip-spec.md Section 4.2. Do not modify this file without updating the RFC document accordingly.
title: AIP Credential Token Payload
description: The JWT payload of an AIP Credential Token. Defined in AIP-CORE-v0.1 §6.4. The full Credential Token is a compact JWT: base64url(header).base64url(payload).signature. JWT Header MUST contain: typ='AIP+JWT', alg='EdDSA' (REQUIRED) or 'ES256'/'RS256' (OPTIONAL), kid=<did:aip:...#key-n>. This schema validates the decoded payload only.

aip_version.description: AIP protocol version. MUST be "0.1" for this spec.
aip_version.$comment: Increment enum when a new spec version is published.

iss.description: AID of the agent issuing and signing this token. MUST match the AID identified by the kid header field. MUST equal the sub field of the last element of aip_chain (verified by Validation Algorithm Step 8 Post-Check A).

sub.description: AID of the agent this token represents. For non-delegated tokens (aip_chain length 1), MUST equal iss (verified by Step 8 Post-Check B). For delegated tokens (chain length > 1), iss and sub may differ — iss is the signing agent, sub is the agent on whose behalf the token is issued.

oneOf.description: Single audience — the relying party's identifier.
Multiple audiences. MUST include the relying party's identifier.

aud.description: Intended audience. The relying party MUST reject any token where its own identifier is not present in this field (Validation Algorithm Step 5d). Tokens with a mismatched aud return invalid_token.

iat.description: Issued-at time as a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). MUST NOT be in the future — tokens with future iat are rejected (Step 5a, clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds).

exp.description: Expiry time as a Unix timestamp. MUST be strictly greater than iat (zero-duration tokens rejected, Step 5b). TTL limits: standard scopes max 3600s; transactions.*, communicate.*, filesystem.execute max 300s. When multiple scope categories are present, the most restrictive TTL applies. Checked in Step 5c (token_expired) and Step 6 (TTL cap).

jti.description: JWT ID — UUID v4 in canonical lowercase hyphenated format. Used for replay prevention. Relying parties MUST maintain a replay cache keyed by (iss, jti) pairs for the token's validity window. Tokens whose (iss, jti) pair has been seen before return token_replayed. Keying by issuer prevents false rejections from UUID collisions across different agents.

items.description: A scope string in dot-notation format (e.g., email.read, transactions, web.browse). Defined scope values listed in AIP-CORE-v0.1 §5.2.

aip_scope.description: Capability scopes requested for this interaction. MUST be a subset of the agent's Capability Manifest (verified in Step 9d using the exhaustive scope-to-manifest mapping). Each scope MUST match a defined identifier from §5.2. Duplicate scope strings are prohibited. Returns insufficient_scope on mismatch.

items.description: A compact-serialised signed JWT (header.payload.signature) encoding one AIP Principal Token conforming to principal-token.schema.json. Each element MUST be a valid JWT whose signature is verifiable using the public key of the party identified by its delegated_by field (or the root principal's public key for index 0). Elements MUST be ordered from root (index 0, delegation_depth=0) to the immediate parent of the acting agent (index n-1).

aip_chain.description: Ordered array of compact-serialised Principal Token JWTs forming the delegation chain. Minimum: 1 element (direct principal delegation). Maximum: 11 elements (corresponding to the hard maximum delegation depth of 10 — depth-10 agent has 11 chain elements including the root). Validated by Validation Algorithm Step 8. Chains exceeding 11 elements return delegation_chain_invalid.

aip_registry.description: OPTIONAL. Advisory URI of the AIP Registry where this agent's public key and revocation status can be verified. Relying parties MAY choose to verify against a different Registry — this field is informational, not authoritative.

jwt_header_requirements.$comment: JWT header requirements for the AIP Credential Token (not validated by this payload schema — header is validated by Validation Algorithm Steps 1-2): typ MUST be 'AIP+JWT'; alg MUST be one of 'EdDSA' (REQUIRED), 'ES256' (OPTIONAL), 'RS256' (OPTIONAL, legacy enterprise only, never sole algorithm); kid MUST be a DID URL in format did:aip:<namespace>:<32hex>#key-<n>.

ttl_rules.$comment: TTL enforcement (AIP-CORE-v0.1 §6.5): standard scopes (email.*, calendar.*, web.*, spawn_agents): max exp-iat = 3600s. Sensitive scopes (transactions.*, communicate.*, filesystem.execute): max exp-iat = 300s. When mixed, most restrictive applies.

dpop_requirement.$comment: DPoP requirement (AIP-SECURITY-v0.1 §5.1): when aip_scope contains transactions, communicate.*, or filesystem.execute, a DPoP proof JWT MUST be present in the DPoP HTTP header. DPoP proofs MUST use EdDSA regardless of the Credential Token alg. DPoP proof jti replay cache is separate from Credential Token jti cache and is keyed by (kid, jti).

$comment: Credential Tokens are JWTs — signing uses standard JWT compact serialisation, not custom canonicalization. See AIP-CORE-v0.1 §6 for header requirements, §6.5 for TTL table, §7 for the full 12-step Validation Algorithm, and AIP-SECURITY-v0.1 §5 for DPoP details.
